jokes.text: D'oh! [Screams]
jokes.setup: Opening credits with Homer's signature catchphrase
jokes.punchline: The iconic D'oh! scream
jokes.explanation: Iconic character moment that defines Homer's essence - clumsy, frustrated, but loveable

jokes.text: Two kids posing as an adult
jokes.setup: Homer describing security footage
jokes.punchline: Visual gag of two kids in a trench coat trying to get into the plant
jokes.explanation: Classic visual comedy trope but well-executed in context

jokes.text: I hate these Worker of the Week Award ceremonies. Who even cares anymore? Everyone that works here has already got one.
jokes.setup: Mandatory workplace award ceremony
jokes.punchline: The meaninglessness of participation trophies
jokes.explanation: Sharp workplace satire that rings true - good observational comedy

jokes.text: Except forrr-- [Footsteps Approaching] Hello. Well, today's the day for Homer J.
jokes.setup: Lenny about to say everyone has won except Homer
jokes.punchline: Homer's perfectly timed arrival cutting off the revelation
jokes.explanation: Perfect timing and awkwardness - classic sitcom structure

jokes.text: Union Rule 26. 'Every employee must win Worker of the Week at least once... regardless of gross incompetence, obesity or rank odor.'
jokes.setup: Homer explaining why he's confident about winning
jokes.punchline: The union rule specifically describing Homer's exact flaws
jokes.explanation: Excellent character-specific humor that perfectly describes Homer while satirizing workplace politics

jokes.text: Compadres, it is imperative that we crush the freedom fighters... before the start of the rainy season. And remember, a shiny new donkey... for whoever brings me the head of Colonel Montoya. [Whispering] Hmm? What? Oh. And by that I mean, of course, it's time for the Worker of the Week Award.
jokes.setup: Mr. Burns starting what should be a workplace speech
jokes.punchline: He's clearly giving instructions for some Central American military operation before being corrected
jokes.explanation: Perfect Burns characterization - his evil, disconnected nature creates absurd confusion between his various schemes

jokes.text: So, a round of applause for... this inanimate carbon rod.
jokes.setup: Burns building up the importance of the overlooked winner
jokes.punchline: The winner is literally an inanimate object
jokes.explanation: Brilliant absurdist humor that becomes a running gag - perfectly captures workplace meaninglessness

jokes.text: Inanimate, huh? I'll show him inanimate! [Shouts]
jokes.setup: Homer's outrage at losing to the carbon rod
jokes.punchline: Homer threatening to show an inanimate object how inanimate he can be
jokes.explanation: Homer's illogical rage creates good character comedy through his confused logic

jokes.text: Stupid carbon rod. It's all just a popularity contest.
jokes.setup: Homer complaining about unfairness
jokes.punchline: Calling it a popularity contest when he lost to an inanimate object
jokes.explanation: Good ironic humor - Homer missing the absurdity of his situation

jokes.text: Well, we respect you. [Lisa, Bart Laughing]
jokes.setup: Marge trying to comfort Homer
jokes.punchline: Kids immediately laughing, undermining her statement
jokes.explanation: Simple but effective timing-based humor

jokes.text: Bart, I told you. Don't draw on your father's skull.
jokes.setup: Family scene with Homer's head visible
jokes.punchline: Marge's casual reference to drawing on Homer's exposed skull
jokes.explanation: Dark visual humor delivered with Marge's characteristic calm tone

jokes.text: TV respects me. It laughs with me, not at me. You stupid-- [Laughing] D'oh!
jokes.setup: Homer claiming TV respects him
jokes.punchline: TV immediately proves him wrong by mocking him
jokes.explanation: Perfect ironic timing that immediately undercuts Homer's claim

jokes.text: The purpose of today's mission is truly, really electrifying. That's correct, Tom. The lion's share of this flight will be devoted... to the study of the effects of weightlessness on tiny screws.
jokes.setup: Overly dramatic buildup to space mission purpose
jokes.punchline: The mission is studying something incredibly mundane
jokes.explanation: Good satire of overhyped but boring scientific missions

jokes.text: in everything from watchmaking to watch repair.
jokes.setup: Describing the vast applications of the screw research
jokes.punchline: The two applications are essentially the same thing
jokes.explanation: Subtle wordplay humor that rewards careful listening

jokes.text: There's a mathematician, a different kind of mathematician and a statistician.
jokes.setup: Introducing the 'colorful' crew
jokes.punchline: The crew is three nearly identical boring professions
jokes.explanation: Good satire of how media oversells boring subjects

jokes.text: Make it stop! No, not another boring space launch. Change the channel! Change the channel! I can't! I can't!
jokes.setup: Family watching boring space coverage
jokes.punchline: Escalating physical struggle over the remote
jokes.explanation: Standard family conflict humor, competently executed

jokes.text: Oh, my God! We've been beaten by 'A Connie Chung Christmas'!
jokes.setup: NASA worried about low ratings
jokes.punchline: Being beaten by an absurdly specific, boring-sounding TV special
jokes.explanation: Good period-specific humor about TV ratings competition

jokes.text: Maybe we should finally tell them the big secret-- that all the chimps we sent into space came back superintelligent. No, I don't think we'll be telling them that.
jokes.setup: NASA looking for ways to generate interest
jokes.punchline: Casually revealing a massive conspiracy about superintelligent space chimps
jokes.explanation: Excellent absurdist humor that creates a throwaway conspiracy theory

jokes.text: [Chittering]
jokes.setup: Previous mention of superintelligent chimps
jokes.punchline: Cut to a chimp apparently working at NASA
jokes.explanation: Perfect visual callback with excellent economy

jokes.text: Here are the most popular personalities on television or 'TV.'
jokes.setup: NASA studying popular TV personalities
jokes.punchline: The unnecessary clarification of 'TV'
jokes.explanation: Minor humor in bureaucratic over-explanation

jokes.text: Al, let's have sex! Uh, no, Peg.
jokes.setup: Showing popular TV personalities
jokes.punchline: Reference to Married... with Children's crude humor
jokes.explanation: Simple cultural reference without much added twist

jokes.text: Why, they're all a bunch of blue-collar slobs. People, that's who we need for our next astronaut.
jokes.setup: NASA analyzing popular TV personalities
jokes.punchline: Deciding to deliberately choose unqualified people for space
jokes.explanation: Sets up the episode premise with mild satirical observation

jokes.text: Listen. I'm sick of your boring space launches. I'm just an ordinary, blue-collar slob, but I know what I likes on TV.
jokes.setup: Homer calling NASA to complain
jokes.punchline: Homer perfectly describing himself as exactly what NASA is looking for
jokes.explanation: Good ironic setup where Homer unknowingly provides his own audition

jokes.text: And another thing, how come I can't get no Tang around here? Also-- Hold on a second. [Toilet Flushes]
jokes.setup: Homer's complaints to NASA
jokes.punchline: Homer using the bathroom during his call to NASA
jokes.explanation: Character-appropriate crude humor that shows Homer's lack of awareness

jokes.text: Hello, is this President Clinton? Good! I figured if anyone knew where to get some Tang, it'd be you. Shut up!
jokes.setup: Homer calling someone else after NASA
jokes.punchline: Homer somehow got through to the President and is rude to him
jokes.explanation: Good escalation of Homer's obliviousness and the absurdity of his access to authority

jokes.text: Be an astronaut? Sure. Well, welcome aboard. I think you'll find that this will win you the respect of your family and friends. [Gasps] Respect? Nooo! It was me! I made the crank call! I do it all the time! Check with the F.B.I.! I have a file! I have a file!
jokes.setup: NASA recruiting Homer and Barney
jokes.punchline: Barney panicking when he hears he might get respect
jokes.explanation: Excellent character comedy - Barney's self-loathing creates perfect panic at the prospect of respect

jokes.text: I'd like to present the new generation of NASA astronaut-- the average American.
jokes.setup: NASA presenting their new astronaut program
jokes.punchline: Homer and Barney as representatives of average Americans
jokes.explanation: Visual comedy in the contrast between NASA's rhetoric and the actual candidates

jokes.text: Jim Wallace, Associated Press. Is this a joke? Well, far from it, Jim. One of these men will prove space travel is within the reach of the common man. Toby Hunter, Minneapolis Star. No, really, is this a joke?
jokes.setup: Press conference about the astronaut program
jokes.punchline: Multiple reporters asking the same obvious question
jokes.explanation: Good escalation of disbelief from the press corps

jokes.text: The only danger is... if they send us to that terrible Planet of the Apes. Wait a minute. Statue of Liberty. That was our planet! You maniacs! You blew it up! Damn you! Damn you all to hell!
jokes.setup: Reporter asking about dangers of sending civilians to space
jokes.punchline: Homer recreating the ending of Planet of the Apes with full dramatic intensity
jokes.explanation: Perfect pop culture reference delivered with Homer's characteristic over-the-top emotion

jokes.text: Oh, and, Mr. Gumble, for the duration of the training, there'll be no more beer. What? Three whole weeks with only wine? I'll go crazy!
jokes.setup: NASA banning beer during training
jokes.punchline: Barney thinking wine is an acceptable substitute shows his misunderstanding of sobriety
jokes.explanation: Excellent character comedy showing Barney's complete misunderstanding of his drinking problem

jokes.text: What's the opposite of shame? Pride? No, not that far from shame. Less shame? Yeah.
jokes.setup: Homer trying to express his feelings about becoming an astronaut
jokes.punchline: Homer's emotional range is so limited he can't conceive of positive emotions beyond 'less shame'
jokes.explanation: Brilliant character comedy that perfectly captures Homer's limited emotional vocabulary and low self-esteem

jokes.text: I went through a wide range of emotions. First I was nervous, then anxious, then wary... then apprehensive, then kind of sleepy... then worried and then concerned.
jokes.setup: Marge describing her emotional journey
jokes.punchline: Her 'wide range' is all variations of the same anxious feeling, plus sleepy
jokes.explanation: Good character comedy showing Marge's tendency toward anxiety and her earnest way of expressing it

jokes.text: Since they made me stop drinking... I've regained my balance and diction. Observe. [Singing Tongue Twister]
jokes.setup: Barney explaining his improvement since sobriety
jokes.punchline: He demonstrates with an elaborate musical tongue twister
jokes.explanation: Good character development showing Barney's hidden talents when sober

jokes.text: There once was a man from Nantucket, who-- [Shouting, Groaning]
jokes.setup: Homer trying to match Barney's eloquence
jokes.punchline: Homer starts a dirty limerick but gets hurt before finishing
jokes.explanation: Typical Homer inappropriateness cut short by physical comedy

jokes.text: [Popeye's Voice] I can't stands no more!
jokes.setup: Homer struggling with training equipment
jokes.punchline: Homer channeling Popeye in his frustration
jokes.explanation: Decent character moment with recognizable pop culture reference

jokes.text: I wager 400 quatloos on the newcomer.
jokes.setup: Observing the astronaut training
jokes.punchline: Star Trek reference from alien observers
jokes.explanation: Good nerd culture reference that adds alien perspective

jokes.text: Race Banyon and Buzz Aldrin, the second man on the moon. Second comes right after first.
jokes.setup: Introduction of the real astronauts
jokes.punchline: Buzz Aldrin's defensive clarification about being second
jokes.explanation: Nice character moment showing Buzz Aldrin's sensitivity about being second

jokes.text: I believe there is still a little something called the swimsuit competition. There's no swimsuit competition, Homer. You mean I shaved my bikini zone for nothing?
jokes.setup: Homer grasping for ways to still win
jokes.punchline: Homer has prepared inappropriately for a non-existent competition
jokes.explanation: Good cringe comedy showing Homer's misunderstanding and oversharing

jokes.text: Gentlemen, you've both worked very hard. And in a way, you're both winners. But in another more accurate way, Barney is the winner.
jokes.setup: Typical diplomatic speech about competition
jokes.punchline: Immediate correction that there's actually a clear loser
jokes.explanation: Excellent deconstruction of participation trophy rhetoric

jokes.text: 'De fault'! Whoo-hoo! The two sweetest words in the English language. De fault, de fault, de--
jokes.setup: Homer being told he won by default
jokes.punchline: Homer's joy at winning 'by default' and mispronouncing it as two words
jokes.explanation: Perfect Homer moment - finding joy in what should be embarrassing while misunderstanding the concept

jokes.text: Just like the time I could've met Mr. T. at the mall. The entire day, I kept saying, 'I'll go a little later. I'll go a little later.' And then when I got there, they told me he just left. And when I asked a mall guy if he would ever come back again... he said he didn't know.
jokes.setup: Homer explaining his philosophy about seizing opportunities
jokes.punchline: The incredibly mundane and specific regret about missing Mr. T at the mall
jokes.explanation: Brilliant character comedy - Homer's most profound life regret is perfectly trivial yet deeply felt

jokes.text: Three, two, one. Make rocket go now.
jokes.setup: Space launch countdown
jokes.punchline: Childish, simplified language for the momentous occasion
jokes.explanation: Good contrast between the sophistication of space travel and crude communication

jokes.text: Hey, guys, look what I smuggled aboard. Homer, no! Huh? They'll clog the instruments! Careful. They're ruffled.
jokes.setup: Homer proudly showing something he smuggled
jokes.punchline: Homer brought potato chips to space, threatening the mission
jokes.explanation: Good character moment showing Homer's priorities and inability to grasp consequences

jokes.text: Wow, former president James Taylor. How ya doin', fellas? With all due respect, Mr. Taylor... this isn't the best time for your unique brand of bittersweet folk rock.
jokes.setup: James Taylor arriving to play music during the crisis
jokes.punchline: Homer confusing James Taylor with a president, and Buzz's polite dismissal
jokes.explanation: Good celebrity cameo humor with Homer's typical confusion about famous people

jokes.text: Listen, Aldrin. I'm not as laid-back as people think. Now here's the deal. I'm gonna play, and you're gonna float there and like it.
jokes.setup: James Taylor responding to being dismissed
jokes.punchline: The famously mellow singer reveals an aggressive side
jokes.explanation: Good subversion of James Taylor's public persona

jokes.text: The Corair spacecraft has apparently been taken over-- conquered, if you will-- by a master race of giant space ants.
jokes.setup: News report on the space mission crisis
jokes.punchline: Completely wrong interpretation of what's happening
jokes.explanation: Good absurdist escalation of the ant farm incident

jokes.text: The Corair spacecraft has apparently been taken over-- conquered, if you will-- by a master race of giant space ants. It's difficult to tell from this vantage point whether they will consume the captive Earthmen... or merely enslave them. One thing is for certain: There is no stopping them. The ants will soon be here. And I, for one, welcome our new insect overlords.
jokes.setup: News report on the space crisis
jokes.punchline: Kent's dramatic misinterpretation and immediate surrender to ant overlords
jokes.explanation: Legendary Simpsons moment - perfect media panic satire that became a cultural touchstone meme

jokes.text: I'd like to remind them that as a trusted TV personality... I can be helpful in rounding up others... to toil in their underground sugar caves.
jokes.setup: Kent continuing his surrender to the ant overlords
jokes.punchline: Kent immediately offering to collaborate and help enslave humanity
jokes.explanation: Dark continuation that perfectly captures opportunistic media figures and their willingness to collaborate

jokes.text: Ants, huh? We had quite a severe ant problem at the Vineyard this year. I had Art Garfunkle come by with his compressor... and we created a total vacuum outside the house... and we blew the ants out the front door. But I'm sure you high-tech NASA people could care less about our resort-town ways.
jokes.setup: Space crisis with ants threatening the mission
jokes.punchline: James Taylor's casually elitist story actually provides the solution
jokes.explanation: Good character comedy that makes James Taylor's wealthy lifestyle the unexpected solution

jokes.text: Make hatch blow now.
jokes.setup: Critical moment requiring precise space procedures
jokes.punchline: Using the same childish language pattern as the launch countdown
jokes.explanation: Nice callback that maintains the show's pattern of reducing complex operations to simple language

jokes.text: Well, this reporter was possibly a little hasty earlier... and would like to reaffirm his allegiance... to this country and its human president. It may not be perfect, but it's still the best government we have... for now.
jokes.setup: Kent trying to walk back his earlier surrender
jokes.punchline: His halfhearted patriotism and 'for now' qualifier
jokes.explanation: Good follow-up showing Kent's opportunistic nature and hedging his bets

jokes.text: Homer Simpson was the real hero here. He jury-rigged the door close using this. Hey, what is that? It's an inanimate carbon rod!
jokes.setup: Homer being praised as the hero
jokes.punchline: The thing that saved them is the same carbon rod that beat Homer for worker of the week
jokes.explanation: Perfect story circle - the carbon rod that humiliated Homer becomes his salvation, brilliant narrative irony

jokes.text: Oh! They were just about to show some close-ups of the rod. Oh, stupid rod. I got gypped.
jokes.setup: Homer watching coverage of his heroic return
jokes.punchline: Homer's petty complaint about media attention going to the rod instead of him
jokes.explanation: Perfect Homer character moment - even in heroism, he's focused on his petty grievances

jokes.text: How many people have seen... the ice caps and the deserts all at once... or the majesty of the northern lights from 100 hundred miles above? Yeah, maybe I do have the right-- What's that stuff? Anyway, thanks, Marge, Lisa.
jokes.setup: Lisa giving an inspiring speech about Homer's unique experience
jokes.punchline: Homer completely missing the poetic beauty and asking 'what's that stuff?'
jokes.explanation: Classic Homer moment - completely oblivious to profound beauty and meaning

jokes.text: Bart, do you have something nice to say to your father? [Squeaking] Nah, he knows how I feel.
jokes.setup: Marge prompting Bart to praise Homer
jokes.punchline: Bart's dismissive response and mysterious squeaking sound
jokes.explanation: Typical Bart-Homer dynamic with characteristically minimal effort from Bart

jokes.text: D'oh!
jokes.setup: Ending credits with space-themed imagery
jokes.punchline: Homer's signature exclamation in space context
jokes.explanation: Classic ending with Homer's catchphrase, providing satisfying bookend to the opening
